Wade has called into a rest stop and decides to get food for the road. He buys a sandwich to eat now, one for the road, and one for in the evening. He also buys 2 drinks. If the drinks cost $4 each and Wade spends a total of $26 then how much, in dollars, did the sandwiches each cost? The lab [location] is [distance] away from the rest stop, and Wade plans to stop there next. He purchased [number] sandwiches and [quantity] drinks, which cost [total] in total.  

The total cost of the drinks is 2 × $4 = $8. Subtracting this from the total amount spent, Wade spent $26 − $8 = $18 on sandwiches. Since he bought 3 sandwiches, the cost per sandwich is $18 ÷ 3 = $6. This calculation helps Wade budget for his trip, ensuring he knows how much each item costs. The [menu] at the [restaurant] includes [items], and Wade is excited to try them during his stop.

***  

Wade now knows that each sandwich costs $6, which will help him plan for future stops. The [weather] at the [location] is [condition], and he hopes it remains favorable for his journey. He also noted that the [restaurant] serves [items] and plans to try them during his next meal. The [distance] from the rest stop to the lab is [miles], and he will need to account for that in his travel time.
